title: Feds release teen detained by ICE in Marlborough
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: A 14-year-old Brazilian girl detained by ICE in Marlborough was released by a federal judge and reunited with her aunt.
The Department of Homeland Security claims she was not with legal guardians and rescued from gang affiliates in Massachusetts.
Attorney Andrew Lattarulo aims to secure her legal residency amid indications the government may attempt deportation.

title: With ‘top guys’ struggling, a lineup reshuffle might be needed for Bruins
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Bruins' top players are underperforming, threatening the team's playoff hopes as they cling to a wild-card spot.
Coach Marco Sturm considers reshuffling lines, with success seen from Pastrnak alongside younger players Minten and Khusnutdinov.
Power play efficiency has dropped, with the team converting only 14.3% of power plays over the last eight games.

title: Swiss student came to Boston to study rehab. Now he’s running for trauma survivors.
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Alexis Cogne, a Swiss researcher at Harvard Medical School, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for trauma survivors.
Cogne aims to raise $10,000 for the Stepping Strong Center at Brigham and Women's Hospital to support trauma rehabilitation.
Cogne's effort highlights the need for more funding in trauma research and rehabilitation, inspired by a recent Swiss fire tragedy.

title: Fire destroys Newbury home, kills pets as family escapes
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A fire destroyed a Newbury home early Friday morning, killing a dog and cat, but residents escaped unharmed.
The blaze at 49 Fruit St. prompted a second alarm response as additional crews were called to battle the flames.
Newburyport, Salisbury, and other local fire departments assisted, while police investigate the fire's origin and cause.

title: How a Cambridge home cook built a 1,000-member recipe club around local ingredients
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: Andrew Little founded the Cambridge Recipe Club to focus on local ingredients and community-centered cooking in Massachusetts.
The club, with over 1,000 members, offers weekly newsletters featuring recipes using seasonal local produce.
Member collaboration is encouraged, with community recipes and input from local chefs enriching the recipe offerings.

title: Campbell to continue suit against Live Nation, as internal messages show employee mocked customers
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Massachusetts AG Andrea Campbell continues antitrust lawsuit against Live Nation despite DOJ settlement agreement.
Live Nation's internal Slack messages reveal mocking of customers by employee Ben Baker over VIP ticket sales.
Live Nation controls over 265 venues in North America, with Ticketmaster handling 80% of primary concert ticketing.

title: Healey, Campbell launch portal for reporting ICE misconduct
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Gov. Maura Healey and AG Andrea Campbell launched a portal for reporting ICE misconduct in Massachusetts.
The portal allows residents to submit photos, videos, and reports of alleged misconduct by federal immigration agents.
Healey and Campbell, critics of Trump's policies, aim to identify misconduct patterns and inform potential legal action.

title: Jaylen Brown criticizes NBA officials for allowing foul-baiting, rewarding flopping
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jaylen Brown criticizes NBA officiating for rewarding foul-baiting and flopping after loss to Oklahoma City.
Brown questions the integrity of the game, feeling pressured to flop to receive favorable calls.
Celtics' Jaylen Brown played 41 minutes with a game-high 34 points against Oklahoma City.

title: Oscars 2026 predictions: Full list of who will win each category
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: The 2026 Oscars, hosted by Conan O'Brien, will air this Sunday on ABC at 7 p.m.
Predictions favor 'One Battle After Another' for Best Picture and Michael B. Jordan for Best Actor.
Paul Thomas Anderson is expected to win Best Director for 'One Battle After Another'.

title: Why new Patriots safety Kevin Byard predicted New England’s breakthrough season
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Kevin Byard joins New England Patriots on a one-year, $9 million contract to strengthen their secondary.
Byard's decision was influenced by reuniting with coach Mike Vrabel, emphasizing Vrabel's leadership capabilities.
Byard led the NFL with seven interceptions last year and has never missed a game in his 10-year career.
